$comment: c85 (shell.c POSIX env surface): setenv/unsetenv ship from stdlib.json gated [elf,macho] (POSIX, not C89 — the realpath precedent; msvcrt has only _putenv) + chdir from unistd.json (flat, rides the header [elf,macho] gate). glibc-verified: prototypes /usr/include/{stdlib,unistd}.h, exports nm -D libc.so.6 W setenv/W unsetenv/W chdir on x86_64 AND aarch64 (one version each). The witness: a full setenv->getenv ROUND-TRIP through the real libc environ (byte-equal value), the overwrite flag BOTH ways (0 preserves, 1 replaces — proves the third int arg is ABI-wired), unsetenv->getenv==NULL, and chdir("/")+getcwd reading back exactly "/" (the cwd really moved). Every branch exits with a DISTINCT code — attributable, never silent. ELF LEGS ONLY: pe does not ship setenv (honest S0001 there); the macho gate is header-verified but that leg runs only in CI — no macho target here (the stdio_stream_objects precedent). RED-ON-DISABLE witnessed: un-ship setenv -> S0001 at the call. gcc -std=c17 cross-checked in WSL (exit 42, same stdout, both arches).
